steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "Now, we know that ______." The phrase we know that introduces a noun clause functioning as the object of know. Because the main sentence is a statement, the noun clause must use declarative (subject-verb) word order and end with a period, not a question mark.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — was the author's real name Anne Brontë?: Inverted word order (verb before subject) turns the clause into a question, which is wrong after we know that. ✗
  • Choice B — the author's real name was Anne Brontë.: Subject before verb (declarative order) and ends with a period — grammatically correct for a noun clause. ✓
  • Choice C — was the author's real name Anne Brontë.: Inverted word order despite the period. The subject-verb inversion still violates the rule for noun clauses. ✗
  • Choice D — the author's real name was Anne Brontë?: Correct word order but ends with a question mark, which contradicts the statement "we know that." ✗
That confirms B is correct.
